items.title: Guard None return from read_json_safe before .get()
items.description: In src/adapters/base.py _decompose_plugin() ~line 544: `mcp_data = read_json_safe(mcp_json)` can return None on parse failure. The next line `servers = mcp_data.get('mcpServers', mcp_data)` then raises AttributeError. This is not caught by `except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError, AdapterError)` so the exception propagates out of _decompose_plugin entirely. Fix: add `if mcp_data is None: return` (or `if not mcp_data:`) immediately after the read_json_safe call at line 543.

items.title: Strip orphaned start marker when end marker is missing
Validate zip entry paths before writing in restore_from_archive
items.description: In src/backup_manager.py restore_from_archive() line 253: `dest = project_dir / entry.replace('home', '~')` does not prevent path traversal. A zip entry named `../../etc/crontab` produces a dest path outside project_dir. Fix: after computing dest, add `dest = dest.resolve(); dest.relative_to(project_dir.resolve())` wrapped in try/except ValueError to skip or raise on any entry that escapes the project directory.

items.title: Guard should_debounce against None state_manager argument
items.description: In src/lock.py should_debounce() line 75: `last_sync = state_manager.last_sync` is called with no prior None check on `state_manager`. Callers that pass None (e.g., early in startup before StateManager is initialised) will get an AttributeError. Fix: add `if state_manager is None: return False` at the top of the function body, before line 75.

items.title: Per-Harness Config Overrides
items.description: Let users create `.claude/overrides/codex.md`, `.claude/overrides/gemini.md`, etc. that get merged on top of CLAUDE.md during sync for that target only. Solves the pain of Codex needing stricter rules or Gemini needing different tool permissions than the global config. Implementation: add `OverrideReader` class in `src/source_reader.py` that checks `.claude/overrides/<target>.md` and merges content after base CLAUDE.md read; each adapter calls it in `src/adapters/base.py` `read_source()` before rendering. Entry point: `/sync` picks it up automatically, `/sync-status` reports which overrides are active.

items.title: Sync Translation Fidelity Report
items.description: Add `/sync-fidelity` command that, without syncing, analyzes your current Claude Code config and shows exactly what will be lost, approximated, or fully preserved per target harness — e.g. 'Codex: 3 MCP servers unsupported, 2 permissions will be dropped, 5 skills will symlink'. Solves the mystery of why a harness behaves differently after sync. Implementation: new `src/commands/sync_fidelity.py` that calls each adapter's `dry_run()` method (add to `base.py`) returning a `FidelityReport` dataclass with `preserved`, `approximated`, `lost` lists; renders as a markdown table per harness. No file writes.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When `/sync` detects a target file was manually edited since last sync, instead of just warning, launch an interactive side-by-side diff in the terminal letting the user pick: keep theirs, keep ours, or merge. Currently users just get a warning and manually fix — this eliminates that friction. Implementation: extend `src/conflict_detector.py` to write a temp file of the incoming content, then `src/commands/sync.py` calls a new `InteractiveResolver` class that uses Python `difflib.unified_diff` to render the diff and prompts for choice via `input()`. Winning content is passed back to the adapter write path. Add `--non-interactive` flag to preserve current behavior for CI.

items.title: Git Pre-Commit Sync Hook Installer
items.description: Add `/sync-git-install` command that installs a git pre-commit hook in the current repo so any commit that touches CLAUDE.md, settings.json, or `.claude/` automatically runs a sync before the commit lands. Solves the problem of committing config changes and forgetting to sync — teammates pull your CLAUDE.md but their harnesses are stale. Implementation: `src/commands/sync_git_install.py` writes `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (or appends to existing) with a shell snippet that calls `python3 src/commands/sync.py --quiet`; checks for existing hook content to avoid double-install; reports installed targets. Uninstall via `/sync-git-install --remove`.

items.title: Live Capability Gap Tracker
items.description: Extend `/sync-matrix` to add a `--watch` flag that monitors your config files with polling and re-renders the capability matrix in the terminal whenever CLAUDE.md or settings.json change — showing in real-time which new settings you added will/won't sync to each harness. Solves the pain of authoring CLAUDE.md blind, not knowing if what you're writing is Gemini/Codex-compatible. Implementation: add `--watch` arg to `src/commands/sync_matrix.py`; use a polling loop (`time.sleep(1)`, compare file mtimes via `os.stat`) to detect changes; on change, clear terminal with `\033[2J` and re-render the matrix table. Works cross-platform via stdlib only.

items.title: Wrap reader.discover_all() in try/except in orchestrator sync_all
items.description: In src/orchestrator.py sync_all() line 248: `source_data = reader.discover_all()` is called with no exception handling. Any OSError or parsing exception during source discovery propagates directly to the caller with no contextual error message. All other major operations in sync_all() (backup, conflict detection, adapter runs) are wrapped. Fix: wrap lines 246-248 in `try/except Exception as e` and re-raise as `SyncError(f'Source discovery failed: {e}')` from `src/exceptions.py` so callers get a clear, actionable message.

items.title: sync_status calls `discover_all()` twice, doubling file-read work
items.description: In src/commands/sync_status.py `_show_default_status` (~lines 319 and 331), `reader.discover_all()` is called twice in the same function: once assigned to `_source_data` and once to `source_data`. Both calls run the full config-discovery pipeline (reading and hashing all CLAUDE.md, settings, skills, agents, commands). Fix: remove the second call at ~line 331 and use `_source_data` directly, saving one full traversal on every `sync-status` invocation.

items.title: Fix backup dir timestamp extraction
items.description: src/backup_manager.py:454-455 — Backup directories are created with the naming format `{filename}_{YYYYMMDD}_{HHMMSS}[_{label}]` (see line 313). The list_snapshots method parses the timestamp via `parts = bdir.name.split('_', 2)` followed by `ts = '_'.join(parts[:2])`. This produces `'{filename}_{YYYYMMDD}'` instead of the actual `'YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS'` timestamp. Fix line 455: replace with `ts = f"{parts[1]}_{parts[2].split('_')[0]}" if len(parts) >= 3 else bdir.name` to correctly extract the date+time portion.

items.title: Remove permanently-dead _tmp_dir cleanup code
items.description: src/orchestrator.py:395 and 467-470 — `_tmp_dir = None` is set inside the `for target in targets:` loop on line 395 and never assigned any other value. The cleanup block at line 467 (`if _tmp_dir and _tmp_dir.exists(): ... _shutil.rmtree(_tmp_dir)`) is therefore permanently dead code — `_tmp_dir` is always None. This is a refactoring remnant from removed temp-directory logic. Remove line 395 and lines 467-470 to eliminate the dead branch and the confusing `_shutil` lazy-import it references.

items.title: `--diff-preview` alone triggers actual rollback, not preview
items.description: In src/commands/sync_rollback.py `_handle_rollback` (~lines 231-238), the condition `elif args.diff_preview and not args.dry_run:` prints 'Proceeding with rollback...' and falls through to execute the restore. The `--diff-preview` flag implies a preview-only operation but actually performs the restore unless `--dry-run` is also passed. Fix: change the branch to print the diff and `return` without restoring, requiring the user to re-run without `--diff-preview` to confirm. Update the argparse help text accordingly.

items.title: Agent file read with `errors='replace'` silently corrupts non-UTF-8 content
items.description: In src/adapters/opencode.py `sync_agents` (~line 283), `agent_path.read_text(errors='replace')` silently replaces non-UTF-8 bytes with the Unicode replacement character (U+FFFD) before writing to `opencode.json`. This corrupts agent instructions without any error or warning. Fix: remove `errors='replace'` so `UnicodeDecodeError` propagates, then add `except UnicodeDecodeError as e: result.failed += 1; log_warning(f'Skipping {agent_path}: encoding error: {e}'); continue` in the surrounding loop.

items.title: Extract magic number 16 to HASH_DIGEST_LENGTH constant in hashing.py
items.description: In src/utils/hashing.py, the truncation `[:16]` appears at lines 37, 48, and 60 — three copies of the same magic number. If the length ever needs to change (e.g., for collision resistance), all three must be updated in sync. Fix: add `HASH_DIGEST_LENGTH = 16` at module level (after imports) and replace all three `[:16]` slices with `[:HASH_DIGEST_LENGTH]`.

items.title: Only increment result.adapted when permissions were actually translated
items.description: In src/adapters/opencode.py sync_settings() line 654: `result.adapted = 1` is set unconditionally even when `permission_config` is empty (no permissions were translated from the source settings). This misreports every settings sync as having adapted something. Fix: move line 654 (`result.adapted = 1`) inside the `if permission_config:` block at line 636 so it only fires when at least one permission entry was produced.

items.title: Guard iterdir() calls with is_dir() in _decompose_plugin
items.description: In src/adapters/base.py _decompose_plugin() lines 498, 513, 528: skills_dir, agents_dir, and commands_dir are passed to .iterdir() without first checking existence. When a plugin metadata flag (has_skills, has_agents, has_commands) is stale or the directory was never created, this raises OSError which is caught but logs a spurious error message to stderr. Fix: add `if not skills_dir.is_dir(): return` (or an equivalent guard before each .iterdir() call) so the OSError path is only hit for genuine permission failures, not missing directories.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.

items.title: MCP Tool Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which MCP tools work in which harnesses and flag tools that will silently fail. Users waste hours debugging why an MCP capability works in Claude Code but not in Gemini.

items.title: Guard TransformEngine.load() None return
items.description: src/orchestrator.py:374 — `_transform_engine.has_rules()` is called immediately after `TransformEngine.load(self.project_dir)` on line 373, but `.load()` can return None. When it does, the AttributeError is swallowed by the outer `except Exception` on line 378 and logged as 'Transform engine load failed' rather than passing silently. Fix: change line 374 from `if _transform_engine.has_rules():` to `if _transform_engine and _transform_engine.has_rules():`

items.title: Guard non-dict JSON in get_current_mcp
items.description: src/sync_preview.py:165-166 — `get_current_mcp()` calls `data.get('mcpServers', {})` directly after `json.loads()` with no type check. If the file contains a JSON array, string, or null at the top level, this raises AttributeError. Fix: wrap line 166 with `if isinstance(data, dict):` so the function falls through to `return {}` for non-dict JSON. Same issue at line 181 in `get_current_settings()` where `data.items()` is called unconditionally — add the same isinstance guard there.

items.title: Fix drift root-cause heuristic priority
items.description: src/drift_detector.py:164-173 — The whitespace-only check (condition 1, line 165: `stored_content.strip() == current_content.strip()`) runs before the version-string check (condition 2, line 170). When a harness self-update changes only indentation or trailing newlines alongside version tokens, the file is misclassified as 'Whitespace normalization' with the wrong suggested action instead of 'Harness self-update'. Fix: swap conditions 1 and 2 so the version-string `elif` (line 170) becomes the first branch, and the whitespace check becomes `elif` after it.

items.title: Validate _pending_rules_files type before use
items.description: src/adapters/codex.py:141-143 — `effective_rules_files = rules_files or getattr(self, '_pending_rules_files', None)` retrieves a dynamically-set attribute without validating its type. `_write_subdirectory_agents_md` at line 148 declares `rules_files: list[dict]` and iterates it internally. If `_pending_rules_files` is set to a non-list truthy value, the downstream iteration crashes. Fix: change line 142 from `if effective_rules_files:` to `if effective_rules_files and isinstance(effective_rules_files, list):`

items.title: Scope-guard am in _show_default_status
items.description: src/commands/sync_status.py:212-223 — `am` is assigned inside a try/except block on line 215, but `am.list_accounts()` is called on line 223 outside that block. If `AccountManager()` raises on line 215, the except swallows it and `has_accounts` stays False — but if `has_accounts` is somehow True from a prior stale state and the assignment failed, line 223 raises NameError. Fix: change line 220 from `if has_accounts:` to `if has_accounts and 'am' in dir():` or more robustly initialise `am = None` before the try-block on line 212 and change line 220 to `if has_accounts and am is not None:`

items.title: Wrap reader.discover_all() in try/except in orchestrator sync_all
items.description: In src/orchestrator.py sync_all() line 248: `source_data = reader.discover_all()` is called with no exception handling. Any OSError or parsing exception during source discovery propagates directly to the caller with no contextual error message. All other major operations in sync_all() (backup, conflict detection, adapter runs) are wrapped. Fix: wrap lines 246-248 in `try/except Exception as e` and re-raise as `SyncError(f'Source discovery failed: {e}')` from `src/exceptions.py` so callers get a clear, actionable message.

items.title: Pre-Sync Visual Diff
items.description: Before any sync runs, show a color-coded side-by-side diff of what will change in each target file. Right now users sync blindly and only discover problems after the fact. This lets them catch translation mistakes, accidental overwrites, or dropped settings before they happen.

items.title: Harness-Specific Rule Overrides
items.description: Let users annotate rules with harness-specific behavior inline, e.g. `<!-- cursor-only -->` or `<!-- skip:aider -->`. Power users need different rules for different harnesses (e.g. stricter rules for Cursor which has real-time suggestions). Currently they have to maintain separate files.

items.title: Named Sync Profiles
items.description: Save and switch between named sync profiles (e.g. 'work-strict', 'personal-experimental', 'client-A'). Each profile specifies which targets, which sections, and any overrides. Solves the pain of developers who consult for multiple clients with different AI policies.

items.title: Harness Config Import Wizard
items.description: When a user adds a new harness that already has an existing config (e.g. an existing GEMINI.md), run a migration wizard that diffs the existing config against what HarnessSync would generate and asks the user which sections to keep, merge, or overwrite. Prevents destroying months of manual work.

items.title: Config Portability Score
items.description: After reading source config, show a portability score (0-100) and per-section breakdown: which rules translate cleanly, which are approximate, and which are dropped entirely. Helps users understand what fidelity they're getting and motivates them to write more portable rules.

items.title: Drift Push Notifications
items.description: When a target harness config has been manually edited since the last sync, surface a clear warning with the exact lines changed. Today users silently diverge and don't realize until something breaks. Pairs with a 'claim these changes' workflow to promote manual edits back into the source.

items.title: Reverse-Promote Target Edits
items.description: When drift is detected, offer a one-command workflow to review and promote changes made in a target harness back into CLAUDE.md. Solves the real-world case where a user fixes something in Cursor's rules directly and wants that fix reflected in the canonical source.

items.title: CI Sync Gate
items.description: A GitHub Actions / CI mode that runs sync in dry-run and fails the build if targets would drift. Teams with multiple contributors can enforce 'all harness configs stay in sync with CLAUDE.md' as a merge requirement, preventing config drift from landing in main.

items.title: MCP Server Compatibility Checker
items.description: Before syncing MCP server configs, probe each target harness to verify it actually supports the MCP protocol version, transport type, and tool names being synced. Saves users from the confusing silent failure of syncing an MCP config that the target harness ignores.

items.title: Rule Usage Analytics
items.description: Track which rules/skills are referenced in conversations across harnesses (via log parsing or hooks) and surface 'dead rules' that haven't been triggered in 30+ days. Helps users prune bloated CLAUDE.md files that slow down every conversation context load.

items.title: Section-Level Sync Targeting
items.description: Let users sync only specific sections to specific harnesses. E.g. 'sync MCP servers to all, but sync security rules only to Cursor and Codex, skip personal workflow rules for Gemini'. Today it's all-or-nothing per target, which forces users to either over-share or under-share.

items.title: Rule Template Library
items.description: A curated library of portable CLAUDE.md rule snippets for common use cases (Python strict mode, no-mock testing, commit message format, language-specific conventions). Users can browse, preview harness translations, and inject directly into their source config.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable changelog of every sync event: what changed, which targets were updated, what was skipped. Right now sync is a black box. A changelog lets users audit history, understand regressions, and answer 'wait, when did that rule change?'

items.title: Config Size Impact Analyzer
items.description: Measure and report how your CLAUDE.md/rules size affects estimated context usage and startup cost in each target harness. Some harnesses load all rules into every prompt — a 50KB CLAUDE.md is expensive. This helps users right-size their configs and understand real performance tradeoffs.

items.title: Auto-Generate Harness Changelogs
items.description: When syncing, auto-append a '## Recent Changes' section to target configs summarizing what changed since last sync (git-powered). Harness users who don't look at the source can see 'added Python type checking rule, updated MCP timeout' without needing to read the diff.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When both source and target have changed since last sync, launch an interactive 3-way merge interface in the terminal (like git mergetool). Today HarnessSync either overwrites or refuses to sync — neither is right. Users need a way to reconcile conflicts without losing work.

items.title: Sync Failure Alerts via Webhook
items.description: Post sync failures, drift warnings, and safety blocks to a Slack/Discord webhook. Teams running HarnessSync in CI or on shared machines need to know when syncs break without watching logs. A simple webhook integration surfaces these events where the team already lives.

items.title: Native Format Preview
items.description: For any target harness, show exactly what the final translated file will look like before writing it. Not a diff — the full rendered output. Useful when onboarding a new harness or debugging why a rule doesn't seem to work in Cursor but works in Codex.

items.title: Per-Project Sync Exclusions
items.description: Some projects have sensitive CLAUDE.md rules (client-specific, proprietary tooling) that shouldn't sync to all harnesses. Add per-project .harnesssync-ignore patterns so users can exclude specific rules/sections project-by-project without touching global config.

items.title: Change Impact Analysis
items.description: Before writing a CLAUDE.md change, show which harnesses will be affected, which sections will need re-translation, and which features will be lost/gained. Helps users understand the downstream blast radius of a config change before committing it.

items.title: Capability Gap Explainer
items.description: For each harness, maintain a living 'what this harness can't do' page showing exactly which Claude Code features have no equivalent, which are approximated, and which are planned. Users constantly discover gaps by accident — surfacing this upfront sets accurate expectations.

items.title: Target Config Version Pinning
items.description: Let users pin specific harnesses to a specific commit/snapshot of the source config. Useful when a harness is on a client machine or production environment where you want stability while continuing to iterate on the source. Decouples sync from deploy.

items.title: First-Run Setup Wizard
items.description: A guided `/sync-setup` that detects which harnesses are installed, shows what HarnessSync found in the source, previews the initial sync for each target, and asks for confirmation. New users currently have to read docs to understand what will happen — the wizard makes it safe and clear.

items.title: Environment Variable Translator
items.description: Automatically translate Claude Code env var settings to the equivalent vars in each target harness (e.g. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY mapping, proxy settings, timeout vars). Today users manually re-set env vars in each harness. Cross-harness env parity is a common setup pain.

items.title: Weekly Sync Health Digest
items.description: A scheduled weekly report (via terminal output or file) summarizing: sync success rate, targets that drifted, rules that failed to translate, and new harness capabilities available. Helps users who set-it-and-forget-it catch creeping config debt before it becomes a problem.

items.title: User-Defined Adapter Hooks
items.description: Let power users drop a custom Python transform script per harness that runs after the built-in adapter. Handles the 20% of edge cases no generic adapter covers: company-specific naming conventions, proprietary harness forks, or post-processing rules. Extensibility without forking.

items.title: Dry-Run Summary Report
items.description: Improve the dry-run output from a raw list of 'would write X' to a structured summary: N files would change, N files unchanged, N files skipped (why), N warnings, estimated context size impact per harness. Makes dry-run actually useful as a planning tool.

items.title: Inter-Rule Conflict Detector
items.description: Scan rules for semantic conflicts (e.g. 'always use tabs' and 'always use spaces', or 'never use console.log' and 'log all API calls') and flag them before sync. Conflicting rules confuse harnesses and produce inconsistent behavior — catching them at the source is far better than debugging in a target.

items.title: New Harness Onboarding Flow
items.description: A guided `/sync-add-harness` that walks through: detecting the harness install path, showing what will be created, running a trial sync to a temp directory for review, and only writing on explicit confirmation. Adding a new harness today is opaque — this makes it a 60-second safe operation.
